slides.lead: Demo presentation of the template. All text is sample (dummy) content and only serves to showcase the styles.
slides.notes: Opening slide. The event name, a one-line subtitle and the meta row belong here. Highlight a word with em for the accent color.

slides.lead: Five roles, five views on the same goal.
slides.notes: Team slide. Instead of photos it uses initials in a colored circle, so it needs no images.

slides.lead: Three parts, with room for questions between them.
slides.notes: Agenda as three large tiles. Good at the start of a block.

slides.lead: Current fitness, the data and the biggest gaps.
slides.notes: Divider slide between blocks. Large heading on a colored gradient.

slides.lead: Watches, mats and apps capture more than anyone has time to read. Value appears only when you connect it.
slides.footnote: // illustrative slide with an enlarged image
slides.notes: Hero slide: large heading, bold illustration, short caption. Good for a point or a chapter.

items: fewer injuries after adding structured recovery
saved weekly on manual plan tracking
higher output per session after load tuning
bullets: Measure impact, not effort: what matters is the effect on performance.
Start where you already have data and where the pain is felt most.
A small test with a clear metric beats a big plan without one.
slides.notes: KPI dashboard: three cards with an icon, a big number and a label, with supporting bullets below.

slides.lead: The plan does not chase fatigue, it predicts it.
bullets: Heart rate, sleep and effort from key sessions.
What preceded past overuse and slumps.
Before form drops or injury shows up.
Recovery into the window when it costs the least.
slides.notes: Two-column slide: bullets with a strong lead word on the left, illustration on the right. Text scales itself up.

slides.lead: The same athlete, two approaches and two outcome curves.
cards: Rest only when something hurts. Costly and unpredictable.
Recovery by the calendar. Better, but often too early.
Recovery by actual readiness. Lowest risk.
Key athletes adaptive, the rest scheduled.
slides.notes: A 2x2 grid of cards. Good for comparison or listing options.

slides.lead: Six typical building blocks of smart training.
cards: Live data from athletes in real time, close to the body.
Live dashboards of load, form and outliers.
Warns of unusual fatigue before a coach spots it.
Turns data straight into a weekly summary for staff.
Sleep and stress kept in healthy zones.
A team that understands the tools and trusts them.
slides.notes: A 3x2 grid with icons in each card header. Great for an overview of tools or capabilities.

items: Gains slow down, it pays to nail the basics.
Tools improve fast and reshape set routines.
Automation starts shaping the plan, not just the math.
slides.notes: Numbered cards with small trend charts and an illustration on the right. Ideal for three possible futures.

slides.lead: Five steps, each with a clear output.
slides.notes: Horizontal flow of steps with arrows. Each box has a title and a small caption.

slides.quote: The winner is not the one with the most data, but the one who turns it into a decision the fastest.
slides.by: Sample quote to showcase the style
slides.notes: Quote slide with a bold left rule. Keep the attribution small.

bullets: Every tool has the name of a person who answers for it.
You know in advance how you will judge success.
On critical steps a person always makes the call.
slides.notes: A plain bulleted slide with a strong lead word. Text scales up automatically to fill the area.

slides.lead: A pilot with no metric and no owner ends up in a drawer.
slides.notes: Statement slide: one big sentence centered on the page. Use it for a key message.

slides.lead: What to do this week, this month and this quarter.
slides.notes: Second divider, opens the practical part.

slides.h1: A report from data in 30 seconds.
slides.lead: We switch to the tool and build a weekly summary live.
slides.footnote: // placeholder slide for a demo or break
slides.notes: Placeholder slide. Centered heading, ideal for a live demo, a break or questions.
Closing slide in the style of the title slide. Contact and call to action.
